title: CSS Tooltip with Arrow
description:
Write CSS to create a **hover tooltip** with a triangular arrow, using only CSS (no JavaScript).

### Visual Target

```
         ┌───────────────────┐
         │  Tooltip content  │
         └────────┬──────────┘
                  ▼
           [Hover me]
```

### Requirements

- `.tooltip` is the wrapper and positioned `relative`
- `.tooltip__content` is hidden by default (`opacity: 0`, `visibility: hidden`)
- On `.tooltip:hover`, the content becomes visible (`opacity: 1`, `visibility: visible`)
- Content is positioned above the trigger, centred horizontally
- Tooltip has background `#1f2937`, white text, `0.5rem 0.75rem` padding, `0.375rem` border-radius
- Tooltip has a small downward-pointing **CSS triangle arrow** (using `::after` pseudo-element)
- Arrow is `6px` borders with `border-top-color: #1f2937`
- Tooltip transitions smoothly (`opacity 0.2s ease`)
- Font size `0.8125rem`, white-space `nowrap`

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Tooltip wrapper | `tooltip` |
| Tooltip content | `tooltip-content` |
| Trigger button | `trigger` |
constraints:
- Only edit styles.css
- Use only CSS for showing/hiding — no JavaScript event handlers
- The arrow must be a CSS triangle (borders technique)
- Must use a pseudo-element (::after) for the arrow
hints: Position .tooltip__content with position: absolute, bottom: 100%, left: 50%, transform: translateX(-50%) to centre it above the trigger.
For the arrow, use ::after with content: '', position: absolute, border-width: 6px, border-style: solid, border-color: #1f2937 transparent transparent transparent.
Add a small margin-bottom (e.g. 0.5rem) to the content so there's a gap between the arrow and trigger. Position the arrow at top: 100% of the content.
